# coolify_database_backup (Resource)

Manages a scheduled database backup configuration on Coolify.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "coolify_s3_storage" "backups" {
  name       = "backup-storage"
  endpoint   = "https://s3.amazonaws.com"
  bucket     = "coolify-backups"
  region     = "us-east-1"
  access_key = var.s3_access_key
  secret_key = var.s3_secret_key
}

resource "coolify_database_backup" "daily" {
  database_uuid = coolify_postgresql_database.db.uuid
  frequency     = "0 2 * * *"
  enabled       = true
  retain_days   = 7
  s3_storage_id = coolify_s3_storage.backups.uuid
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `database_uuid` (String) The UUID of the database to back up. Changing this forces a new resource.
- `frequency` (String) Cron expression for backup schedule (e.g. "0 2 * * *" for daily at 2 AM).

### Optional

- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether the backup schedule is active.
- `retain_days` (Number) Number of backups to keep locally.
- `s3_storage_id` (String) The ID of the S3 storage destination.

### Read-Only

- `id` (Number) The numeric ID of the backup configuration.
- `uuid` (String) The UUID of the backup configuration.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Import a database backup using the composite ID format:
terraform import coolify_database_backup.example <database-uuid>:<backup-id>
```
